title: ‘Artigiani’ e ‘salariati’ nello specchio della società urbana dell’Italia tardo-medievale
highlights: The subject of this contribution is the divergent image that the urban society of late medieval Italy had of craftsmen and wage earners. Although both groups belonged to the broader category of manual workers, the masters enjoyed the esteem that came from their knowledge, from a recognised ‘savoir faire’, from being workshop owners and employers: a  ...    employ themselves as subordinates.
